title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 41 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: New Year's Day
festivals.description: Ireland's official start to the year with parades, celebrations and special events across the country.

festivals.name: Temple Bar Trad Fest
festivals.description: A vibrant celebration of traditional Irish music with sessions, workshops and performances throughout Dublin's iconic Temple Bar area.

festivals.name: Imbolc Festival
festivals.description: An ancient Celtic festival marking the beginning of spring with storytelling, traditional crafts and spiritual ceremonies at historical sites.

festivals.name: St. Brigid's Day
festivals.description: A national celebration of Ireland's patron saint with traditional ceremonies, parades and cultural events nationwide.

festivals.name: Dublin International Film Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious showcase of international and Irish cinema featuring documentaries, shorts and features from around the world.

festivals.name: St. Patrick's Festival
festivals.description: Ireland's most iconic celebration honouring St. Patrick with parades, concerts and festivities throughout the country.

festivals.name: Waterford Festival of Light
festivals.description: A spring celebration featuring light installations, performances and cultural events transforming Waterford's city centre.

festivals.name: Cork St. Patrick's Festival
festivals.description: Cork's vibrant St. Patrick's celebration featuring parades, street parties and cultural performances across the city.

festivals.name: Easter
festivals.description: Ireland's major religious holiday with special masses, family gatherings and Easter egg hunts celebrated nationwide.

festivals.name: Galway International Arts Festival
festivals.description: A renowned two-week celebration of theatre, music, visual arts and comedy attracting international performers and audiences.

festivals.name: Forbidden Fruit
festivals.description: A cutting-edge festival blending electronic music, art installations and performances in Dublin's Herbert Park.

festivals.name: Bealtaine
festivals.description: An ancient Celtic fire festival marking the start of summer with traditional bonfires, rituals and cultural celebrations.

festivals.name: Loughcrew Historic Gardens Spring Fair
festivals.description: A countryside fair celebrating spring with crafts, local produce and historic garden tours in Meath.

festivals.name: Westport Festival
festivals.description: A summer festival featuring traditional Irish music, street entertainment and community celebrations in picturesque Westport.

festivals.name: Bloomsday
festivals.description: A literary festival celebrating James Joyce's Ulysses with guided walks, readings and re-enactments throughout Dublin.

festivals.name: Electric Picnic
festivals.description: Ireland's premier music and arts festival featuring electronic music, theatre and visual art in Laois.

festivals.name: Dún Laoghaire Festival of World Cultures
festivals.description: A vibrant celebration of international cultures with music, dance, food and art along Dublin's coastal promenade.

festivals.name: Kilkenny Arts Festival
festivals.description: A major cultural festival showcasing theatre, music, literature and visual arts in medieval Kilkenny.

festivals.name: Dublin Fringe Festival
festivals.description: An experimental theatre festival featuring avant-garde performances, comedy and innovative arts across Dublin venues.

festivals.name: Rose of Tralee
festivals.description: Ireland's most famous beauty pageant celebrating Irish heritage with a week of pageantry, music and festivities.

festivals.name: Puck Fair
festivals.description: One of Ireland's oldest festivals where a wild goat is crowned king over three days of music, parades and street celebrations.

festivals.name: Fleadh Cheoil na hÉireann
festivals.description: Ireland's premier traditional Irish music festival attracting thousands of musicians and dancers for competitions and sessions.

festivals.name: Dublin Bike Tours Summer Festival
festivals.description: A summer celebration of cycling culture with rides, exhibitions and street parties across Dublin.

festivals.name: Tullamore Dew Irish Whiskey & Bluegrass Festival
festivals.description: A unique blend of Irish whiskey tastings and American bluegrass music in the heart of whiskey country.

festivals.name: Cork Jazz Festival
festivals.description: A world-class jazz festival featuring international and Irish artists performing across Cork venues.

festivals.name: Listowel Writers' Week
festivals.description: A prestigious literary festival honouring Irish writers with readings, workshops and discussions in this literary town.

festivals.name: Samhain
festivals.description: The ancient Celtic festival marking the end of summer with bonfires, costumes and supernatural celebrations nationwide.

festivals.name: Dublin Web Summit
festivals.description: Europe's leading tech conference drawing thousands of entrepreneurs, investors and innovators to Dublin.

festivals.name: All Hallows' Eve
festivals.description: Ireland's most significant Halloween celebrations with parades, haunted tours and traditional Celtic festivities.

festivals.name: Derry Halloween Festival
festivals.description: The world's largest Halloween festival with elaborate costumes, parades and fireworks in historic Derry.

festivals.name: Dunmore East Seafood Festival
festivals.description: A celebration of Ireland's seafood heritage with tastings, cooking demonstrations and traditional fishing village atmosphere.

festivals.name: Galway Oyster Festival
festivals.description: A legendary festival celebrating oyster season with competitions, tastings and celebrations in Galway.

festivals.name: Wexford Festival Opera
festivals.description: Ireland's premier opera festival featuring rare operatic works and world-class performances in historic Wexford.

festivals.name: St. Brigid's Day Celebrations
festivals.description: Traditional celebrations honouring Ireland's patroness with ceremonies and cultural events across the southeast.

festivals.name: Christmas Day
festivals.description: Ireland's major Christian holiday celebrated with family gatherings, church services and festive traditions.

festivals.name: St. Stephen's Day
festivals.description: A national public holiday traditionally marked with sporting events, parades and family celebrations.

festivals.name: Cork Christmas Market
festivals.description: A festive holiday market featuring crafts, food and entertainment in Cork city centre.

festivals.name: Temple Bar Tradfest Winter Sessions
festivals.description: Winter traditional music sessions celebrating Irish folk culture with live performances throughout Dublin.

festivals.name: Sligo Live Festival
festivals.description: A vibrant spring festival of live music and cultural performances in poetry and literature-rich Sligo.

festivals.name: Belfast International Arts Festival
festivals.description: A major autumn festival showcasing theatre, dance, music and visual arts with both traditional and contemporary performances.

festivals.name: Drogheda Town & Country Fair
festivals.description: A traditional summer fair with local crafts, food, music and family entertainment in historic Drogheda.
